speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Beds are clean, tea is questionable. You staying?

speaker: Player
text: Just passing through.

speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Then pass through warm. Wind bites at night.

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Mind the chalk lines—they’re a fierce tournament.

speaker: Player
text: What’s the prize?

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Bragging. Best currency in the yard.

speaker: Juno
text: Courier on a deadline—talk fast or walk with me.

speaker: Player
text: Any news?

speaker: Juno
text: Packages go missing near the docks. Someone’s skimming. Keep your eyes open.

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Papers, please. Ha—kidding. We’re short on papers today.

speaker: Player
text: Anything we should avoid?

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Back alley after midnight. Unless you like buying trouble twice.

speaker: Pasha
text: Step right up—today's lots are honest and only mildly cursed!

speaker: Player
text: We're just looking.

speaker: Pasha
text: Looking is free. Advice, too: bids rise when the drones clock off—late evenings are fierce.

speaker: Rana
text: Watch your step. Half these pallets wobble worse than a drunk loader.

speaker: Player
text: Busy day?

speaker: Rana
text: Always. If a crate's tagged in blue, it’s bound for the mine. Don’t touch blue.

speaker: Sedge
text: You look like someone who can mind a secret.

speaker: Player
text: Depends on the secret.

speaker: Sedge
text: Then it depends on the price. Come back when you’ve got something to trade.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: If you’re bleeding, sit. If you’re not, don’t start.

speaker: Player
text: We’re fine.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: Good. I’m running low on synth-gel and patience in equal measure.

speaker: Sima
text: Noodles hot, pepper hotter. Want steam or fire?

speaker: Player
text: Surprise me.

speaker: Sima
text: Bold. I like bold. Here—don’t breathe in too deep.

speaker: Tallo
text: New faces? Welcome. Roof leaks, hearts don’t.

speaker: Player
text: Nice place.

speaker: Tallo
text: It’s home. The trick is to keep it ours.

speaker: Ellie
text: Just swapping out panels before the sparks get ambitious. What do you need?

speaker: Ellie
text: The arcade's alive again! Here, a little something for keeping the lights bright.

speaker: Jed
text: Nova! Thank the currents. I heard the alarm broadcast. The Warden's security is descending, sealing everything.

speaker: Nova
text: Jed, they're calling me a contaminant. Zeke's overriding the gates, but we have to move now. Come on!

speaker: Jed
text: I can't, girl. The manual override panel for this lift is failing. If I let go of this handle, the lift locks down, and they pin you in this shaft.

speaker: Nova
text: No! We can jam the gears! I'm not leaving you behind!

speaker: Jed
text: This isn't a quota run, Nova. You have to launch. Here—take this Chime. It was in the generator core housing. It's the only thing that will bypass the launch bay locks. Now run, and don't look back.

speaker: Jed
text: You look like a pressure leak with boots. Tell me you slept.

speaker: Nova
text: I slept enough. Quota doesn't care how rested I am.

speaker: Jed
text: Quota also doesn't care if that cutter pops and takes your hand with it. I do.

speaker: Nova
text: If I don't push it, I miss count. If I miss count, I lose scrip. If I lose scrip...

speaker: Jed
text: I know the math. I hate the math. Check your gear, then use the bench. After that, get to shift clearance before Boggs marks you absent. And take the ration bar before you pretend you aren't hungry.

speaker: Jed
text: There you are. Set the Cryo-Inductor on the workbench before that cutter shakes itself apart.

speaker: Nova
text: The Cryo-Inductor? That's the cold-loop part, right?

speaker: Jed
text: Right. Use the workbench to repair it into a Functional Cryo-Inductor. One scrap plate, one steady hand. Then your cutter can survive shift clearance.

speaker: Jed
text: That's the hum. Good. Your cutter can pass a load check without cooking your wrist now.

speaker: Nova
text: So I can work the mine.

speaker: Jed
text: If Dominion clears you. Hear that chime? Every Pit worker reports to Transit Checkpoint before the first cage drops. No clearance, no badge. No badge, no mine floor. No mine floor, no quota or scrip.

speaker: Nova
text: And Boggs marks me absent.

speaker: Jed
text: Exactly. Get to the checkpoint, let Hank's scanner reject whatever it wants, then find Zeke at the Admin window if the system gets stupid. Move.

speaker: Jed
text: Grab a seat if you're sticking around. If something starts smoking, hand me the wrench first.

speaker: Jed
text: You made it back in one piece. That surge settle down once you pulled the core?

speaker: Jed
text: Then we learn what they wanted. I'll crack the housing and see how far the signal goes. Nice work bringing it home.

speaker: Jed
text: Town runs smoother thanks to you. Keep pace like that and we'll stay ahead of the scrappers.

speaker: Jed
text: Stellarium core is cooling on my workbench thanks to you. We'll make good use of it.

speaker: Jed
text: Smell that ozone? That's the core spool. Hand it over before it cooks through your belt.

speaker: Jed
text: Track that pulse to its source. If the deep mine lit up, something below Sector 4 is chewing through power.

speaker: Jed
text: Nice work. That's all of 'em. Now, get this gear back to their owners. They've been pestering me for days.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Quiet feet, please. The little ones are mapping constellations they have never been allowed to see.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Wonderful! The kids will be back under the stars tonight. Please, take this for your trouble.

speaker: Maddie
text: Beans are roasting, and for once the grinder is not threatening to explode. Did you need something?

speaker: Maddie
text: You fixed it! Oh, thank you! Take these credits - may your next cup be on me.

speaker: Nova
text: Burrower was feeding on it. Nest is quiet now, but whoever rigged the chamber knew what they were doing.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Badge.

speaker: Nova
text: Morning to you too.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Morning costs extra. Badge is mandatory. Booth window, then maybe the door likes you.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Keep the line moving. The door doesn't care about your reasons.

speaker: Zeke
text: Hank has to reject your clearance before I can touch the file. Let the scanner make it official, then come back to my window.

speaker: Zeke
text: Name? ID? Clearance sco--oh. It's you. The volunteer.

speaker: Nova
text: I need a mining pass, suit. Shift starts in ten.

speaker: Zeke
text: Right. Eligibility check. Completely routine. Very normal.

speaker: Zeke
text: ...Mandatory Retirement. Asset Liability exceeds projected value. Recycle.

speaker: Nova
text: Recycle means fired, right?

speaker: Zeke
text: It means bio-reclamation. Death. For a breaker surge.

speaker: Zeke
text: I'm flagging it as grid instability. Temporary override. Take the badge before I remember how rules work.

speaker: Zeke
text: The system logged something. I should not have said that out loud.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You opened it? No alarm? No sweep team? Hah. They really did forget the dead.

speaker: Nova
text: They scratched names into the lid.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Then remember them quiet. Loud gets people recycled. Take the grenades and forget who told you.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You want something useful, cutter girl? There's a cache behind the strip. Old resistance stock.

speaker: Nova
text: Why not grab it yourself?

speaker: Scrapper
text: Because I like breathing. Find the crate, open it clean, and bring me proof it wasn't a trap.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Those grenades are loud history. Use them before history uses you.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Keep your voice down. Dominion buys echoes cheaper than scrap.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Buying, selling, or just looking guilty? Keep it quick either way.

speaker: Doc
text: The coughing stopped. Either you fixed the vents or everyone finally died.

speaker: Nova
text: Fixed. Mostly. Dominion's maintenance request is still doing nothing.

speaker: Doc
text: Then this is faster than paperwork. Corrosive rounds. Ugly medicine for armored problems.

speaker: Doc
text: Don't inhale too deep. The vents are backing poison into my patients again.

speaker: Nova
text: Again?

speaker: Doc
text: Dominion denies repairs faster than I can file them. Patch the cracked vent before the ward turns blue.

speaker: Doc
text: Air's still bad, but now it's regular bad. That's what passes for medicine here.

speaker: Doc
text: If you're bleeding, sit. If you're not, don't start.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: You heading down without shield training? That's how assets become floor paste.

speaker: Nova
text: Shield training?

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Loading Dock. Riot shield trainer. Bring the loader online, move my cargo, learn Hydraulic Kick, then go play hero in Sector 4.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: You. Cutter girl. Zeke said you're the one who caused that surge in the Pit.

speaker: Nova
text: It was grid instability, Boggs. I just work the cutters.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Sure it was. Anyway, the interference is overloading the relays in Sector 4. I'm putting you on punishment duty. Descend and clear it.

speaker: Nova
text: Sector 4? That's deep mine. The lift is locked.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: You've got that Mine Access Badge Zeke signed off on. My scanner's updated. Use the elevator, restore power at the Cavern Junction support pillar, then follow the relays until the humming stops.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: You. Cutter girl. Loader's sitting idle and cargo's blocking my schedule.

speaker: Nova
text: There are workers behind that pile.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Then move it faster. Shipment clock doesn't pause for feelings.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Route's clear, workers are breathing, and somehow I'm still behind schedule.

speaker: Foreman Boggs
text: Company time ain't charity. Move.

speaker: Miner Bill
text: Bar's quiet if you ignore the vents. I recommend ignoring the vents.

speaker: The Warden
text: Nova. Asset 942-D. Retinal scan flagged for recycling, grid interference logs, and now unauthorized transit.

speaker: Player
text: We're leaving, Warden. Out of our way.

speaker: The Warden
text: Leave? There is nowhere to go. The dome isn't just physical. It's a lock. And I hold the frequency. You are a standing wave that has lost its pitch. I will quiet you myself.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova! You actually did it. The Warden's down. The way to the pod is clear, but security is going to reboot the grid any second.

speaker: Player
text: Let's move. Jed said the Chime is the key.

speaker: Zeke
text: Get inside the pod! I'll secure the hatch. We need to splice that Chime into the console inside.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova! Connect the Chime to that port on the console. If Jed's math is right, it should establish a resonance that overrides the colony's lock on the pod launchers.

speaker: Player
text: I'm inserting it now. Let's hope the frequency matches.

speaker: Zeke
text: Yes! The core is spooling up. The console's active! We're green for launch. Nova, initiate the sequence on the navigation console!

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova... *cough*... My head feels like a reactor breach. Are you intact?

speaker: Nova
text: I'm fine, Zeke. The pod took the brunt of the impact. How are you holding up?

speaker: Zeke
text: Everything is bruised, but my suit's recycler is still pumping. We're on the surface, Nova. We actually crossed the barrier... but the pod's core is completely fried.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the core housing in the wreckage. If the Chime is still intact, we need to recover it before this swamp water short-circuits it completely.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the wreckage of the pod core. We can't leave the Chime behind.

speaker: Zeke
text: The stream of glow-water seems to flow deeper into the concrete structures. Follow it, Nova. I'll catch my breath and catch up.

speaker: Zeke
text: The beacon's pulse is strong. It's coming from that massive stone gate up ahead. But the path is covered in thick razor-vines. Be careful, Nova.

speaker: Zeke
text: The stasis pod is completely locked down by safety alignment rings. According to the console, it needs a stable chord of 136Hz. That's a C#m7 resonance. Adjust the rings using my frequency broadcast.

speaker: Zeke
text: It worked! The stasis rings are locking into alignment. The pod is venting pressure!

speaker: Zeke
text: No, the rings are still oscillating. The frequency is discordant. Let's try again.

speaker: Zeke
text: Fascinating scans! The cellular structure of these crystalline ferns behaves like an acoustic chamber. Here, take some spare stabilizers for your help.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova, this surface is incredible. The plants here respond directly to resonance frequencies. If you scan 5 unique flora specimens, I can compile an ecological catalog. Can you do that?

speaker: Orion
text: This energy barrier blocks access to the hangar. It vibrates with intense discordance. We must introduce a resolving harmonic lock at C#m7 to bypass it.

options.text: Tune resonator to C# Minor 7 (136.1 Hz)
Tune resonator to E Major (164.8 Hz)

speaker: Orion
text: Perfect! The frequencies match, and the barrier dissolves. Hangar access is clear.

speaker: Orion
text: The barrier hums louder, pushing us back. That frequency is rejected. Let's try again.

speaker: Orion
text: All three crystal voices are seated. Which chord will align the stasis mural and hold each human memory like Ice within the Ocean?

speaker: Orion
text: C#m7. The mural stabilizes: many minds, distinct as Ice, carried without dissolving in the Ocean.

speaker: Orion
text: No, the colors are shifting erratically. The pitch is incorrect.

speaker: Orion
text: Nova, Tideglass Beach is nearby. The wet shore has mineral deposits that make a hot, stabilizing meal. Let's gather 3 tideglass skewers.

speaker: Orion
text: The stasis mural is missing three resonance crystals from the Sanctuary wings. Recover their voices, then we can align the chord without letting the Ocean overwhelm us.

speaker: Orion
text: The current of this place is strong, Nova. I can hear the ocean's resonance.

speaker: Gh0st
text: You do not belong on this frequency. My orders are simple: erase the anomaly.

speaker: Player
text: Wait, we aren't here to fight. The Warden sent us!

speaker: Gh0st
text: The Warden... *static*... error. System logic loop detected. Harmonic lock override...

speaker: Gh0st
text: The transmitter is beyond an active vent security grid. Hack its timing, pass the guard systems without an alert, then retrieve the hardware.

speaker: Gh0st
text: My blades are quiet, but my sensors are alert.

speaker: Zeke
text: Welcome to my safehouse... or what's left of it. They reformatted my life, Nova. Discarded my files, my memories. But the physical terminals... they are still wired.

speaker: Nova
text: We'll take it back, Zeke. For now, Orion, set up the shield. Let's make sure Vale can't hear us.

speaker: Orion
text: Establishing resonance lock. There. The Source is quiet here. They won't detect us.

speaker: Jax
text: Don't ask what's in the drink. Don't ask who was sitting there yesterday. Rules of the house. You looking for elevator passcodes?

options.text: Yes, Zeke said you have the schedules.

speaker: Jax
text: Schedules are on this drive. Keep it out of sight. The Suits are watching.

speaker: Jax
text: The neon sign outside is dead. Can't run a bar in the dark. Bring me a neon core from the street sweepers, and I'll make it worth your while.

options.text: I'll get a core for you.

speaker: Jax
text: You got the core. Install it on the sign console over there.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Nova. Dominion databases at the Transit Plaza might hold records on... Elara. My sister. I cannot access the terminal without triggering security alerts. Will you search it?

speaker: System
text: Downloading Spire security archives... file transfer complete.

speaker: Mika
text: Try the skewers! Real protein today, I promise. No recycled paste. Also... can you help me fix these lanterns? The rain is shorting them out.

speaker: Mika
text: Just redirect the primary power coupling to light them up.

speaker: System
text: Zeke's decryption algorithms are running... blueprints downloaded.

speaker: System
text: All data feeds are connected. The planning table is active.

speaker: Curator
text: Fascinating refraction. Sector 9 lineage?

speaker: Curator
text: A Starborn stray. Control your pet, or I will call security.

speaker: Zeke
text: Sir, this is a commemorative statue. Very lifelike. Move along.

speaker: Zeke
text: Standard Dominion scanner grid. They think nobody smart enough to break it would ever want to leave.

speaker: Zeke
text: You deleted my employee ID? Fine. I am deleting your admin privileges.

speaker: Orion
text: The puzzle is not light. It is pressure in visible form. Tune the quiet frequencies first.

speaker: Orion
text: There. The door is listening now.

speaker: Zeke
text: Wait. Energy density is spiking. If you touch that, the feedback loop lights us up like a flare.

speaker: Nova
text: We do not have time for safe mode. I can tune it out.

speaker: Thorne
text: You just tripped a station-wide audit. Do you have any idea what you have done?

speaker: Thorne
text: He is coming. And he does not leave survivors. I cannot let him find you here.

speaker: Vale
text: Anomaly detected in the Archive. Administrator, lock the district. I want no ungoverned noise leaving Sector 0.

speaker: Concierge
text: Welcome to the altitude where price is proof of citizenship.

speaker: Lab Terminal
text: Prototype trial armed. Warning: liability has been transferred to the test subject.

speaker: Gh0st
text: I know where the vents are. I crawled through them when I was small.

speaker: Zeke
text: These vents are running hot enough to cook a tank. Give me thirty seconds and a reason to hate this place more.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Project Phantom. That name is on the inside of my skull.

speaker: Nova
text: Open it. We face what it says together.

speaker: Gh0st
text: My kill-suite reads Spectral IDs. When you channel the Source, you ring close enough to Elara that the failsafe grabs the wheel.

speaker: Gh0st
text: If I ever draw on you again, use this override key. No hesitation.

speaker: Vale
text: Sector 7 is under audit. Unregistered craft detected. No departures until the anomaly is resolved.

speaker: Gh0st
text: These belts carried bodies before they carried machines. Watch the timing.

speaker: Gh0st
text: They made more of me. Faster. Emptier. Put them down before the line teaches them pain.

speaker: Orion
text: The puzzle is a factory rhythm. Belts, crushers, then silence. Move on the silence.

speaker: Nova
text: It wants to build. I can feel it pulling shape out of me.

speaker: Rylos
text: You are malfunctioning, Unit 734. Submit for correction. Pain is just data leaving the body.

speaker: Vale
text: Commander Rylos. Audit complete. Denied. Director Thorne is relieved. All sectors: lockdown.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Vale locked the Ring to Thorne's old cipher. I know the scar tissue in this code. Give me ten seconds.

speaker: Maintenance Bot
text: SOLAR ARRAY OCCLUDED. HORTICULTURAL LOSSES EXCEED EXECUTIVE PRESENTATION LIMITS.

speaker: Thorne
text: I built the cage. I thought Harmony would be controllable. Sellable. Vale is not trying to own the world. He is trying to erase every voice but his.

speaker: Vale
text: Welcome to the Ring. Director Thorne is unavailable. Orbital Compliance is acting command. For your safety, remain where you are.

speaker: Thorne
text: Three firewall nodes. I designed the maze to keep everyone else out. Vale changed the locks and left me inside it.

speaker: Elara
text: 734. You are late. I cannot hold the door and the firewall. Take the Anchor. Get them out. That is a direct order, brother.

speaker: Vale
text: A choir still contains variance. Variance contains pain. One voice. A solo.

speaker: Zeke
text: I kept telling myself I was surviving the system. I quit. And I do not need to be useful to deserve this family.

speaker: Gh0st
text: I could not save her. I can stop earning my right to exist by bleeding. No more orders.

speaker: Orion
text: They are not dead. They are still in the current. I was frozen. Now I have to remain on the shore a little longer.

speaker: Vale
text: Thorne wanted to bottle the ocean. I will break the glass. No bodies to starve. No minds to break. Why do you struggle to add the friction back?

speaker: Nova
text: You are not saving them. You are building a mass grave and calling the silence peace.

speaker: Sentinel-3
text: [High-pitched tone]... Trading interface established. Select components for exchange.

speaker: Sentinel-3
text: Core status: 89% coherence. Atmospheric humidity: high. The canopy resonance is vibrating at key G minor today.

speaker: Zeke
text: Patrol beacons scatter their signatures across the Wilds. Triangulate all three before trusting the final vector. Their cutter team may have carried something useful.

speaker: Orion
text: Rest is not surrender. The Ocean presses against every thought; food, trusted voices, and a Neural Stabilizer give the Ice time to hold its shape.

speaker: Orion
text: Those razor-vines carry toxic spores. A heated edge could cauterize them as it cuts. The lost patrol transceiver may lead us to one.

speaker: Gh0st
text: Do not touch the transmitter yet. First blind the security grid on the gantry, then cross the access grate between guard sweeps.
